title: IMD issues five-day severe heat warning in Himachal: Plains and lower regions expected to face heatwave con... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
IMD issues five-day severe heat warning in Himachal:Plains and lower regions expected to face heatwave conditions from May 26 onward
Himachal Pradesh is expected to witness intense heat conditions over the next four to five days, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) warning that heatwave conditions may begin affecting the state’s plains and lower regions from May 26 onward.
According to the Meteorological Centre in Shimla, temperatures across several districts are likely to rise by 3°C to 7°C in the coming days.
No major weather alert for May 24 and 25
The weather department has not issued any major alert for most districts on May 24 and 25.
Officials said strong sunlight and reduced rainfall activity during these two days are expected to push daytime temperatures higher across the state.
Residents in the plains may experience hot winds and humid conditions during afternoon hours, making outdoor activities uncomfortable.
Yellow alert for heatwave in several districts
From May 26, heat conditions are expected to intensify further in several parts of the state.
The IMD has issued a yellow alert for heatwave conditions in isolated areas of:
On May 27, heatwave-like conditions may further intensify in:
Weather likely to remain stable in higher regions
The upper hill districts are expected to witness relatively stable weather conditions over the next two days.
No major weather warning has been issued for:
However, temperatures in these regions are also expected to rise gradually.
IMD advises caution during afternoon hours
During the last 24 hours, some parts of the state witnessed light rainfall and thunderstorms, while higher reaches experienced light snowfall, temporarily reducing temperatures.
However, with clearer skies returning, temperatures have started rising once again.
Una has already recorded a temperature of 42°C, making it the hottest place in the state. Temperatures are also steadily increasing in Kangra, Mandi, Bilaspur and Hamirpur districts.
The weather department has advised people to:
- Avoid going out in direct sunlight during afternoon hours
Weather may change again after May 28
According to the IMD, weather conditions in Himachal Pradesh may change after May 28 due to the possible activation of a fresh western disturbance.
Its impact could bring thunderstorms and light rainfall to some parts of the state.

title: Weather Update: Heatwave Kills 16 In Telangana, IMD Alert In Place For Himachal; Will Heat Intensify In Delhi? - The Daily Jagran
full_text:
Weather Update: Several states across the country are reeling under a severe heatwave. On Saturday, the scorching heat and gusty winds pushed temperatures above 44 degrees Celsius in several areas. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned that temperatures are likely to rise further in Delhi, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh from Sunday.
Rohtak in Haryana recorded a maximum temperature of 44.1 degrees Celsius. Bathinda in Punjab reached 43.8 degrees Celsius. Amritsar recorded a maximum temperature of 40.5 degrees Celsius, while Ludhiana recorded a maximum temperature of 41.2 degrees Celsius. Chandigarh recorded a maximum temperature of 40.6 degrees Celsius.
Punjab And Haryana Weather
Punjab Health Minister Dr Balbir Singh has directed government hospitals to start dedicated units for heat stroke management, with active cooling measures including ice packs and necessary medical supplies.
In Haryana, Ambala recorded a maximum temperature of 41.2 degrees Celsius, while Hisar recorded a maximum temperature of 42.4 degrees Celsius.
Jharkhand Weather
The IMD has issued a heatwave alert for three Jharkhand districts - Garhwa, Palamu and Chatra - for Monday, while several other districts are likely to experience thunderstorms, lightning and moderate rainfall over the next six days.
An orange alert has been issued for Dhanbad on Sunday, with rain expected.
Telangana Weather
Telangana Revenue Minister Ponguleti Srinivas Reddy said 16 people have died in seven districts of the state due to the heatwave.
Rajasthan Weather
In Rajasthan, Chittorgarh was the hottest place in the state, reaching 44.2 degrees Celsius in the last 24 hours.

Temperature Crosses 45 Degrees In Delhi
IMD has warned that temperatures are expected to rise further in Delhi-NCR from Sunday. Temperatures have already exceeded 45 degrees Celsius in many areas of Delhi. Hospitals in the National Capital Region (NCR) are seeing a rise in the number of patients suffering from heat-related illnesses.
Himachal Weather
In Himachal Pradesh, the Shimla Meteorological Centre has issued a yellow alert, forecasting a heatwave in parts of the state on May 26 and 27. Districts such as Una, Bilaspur, Hamirpur, Kangra, Solan, and Sirmaur may be affected. Additionally, a western disturbance is expected to bring rain and thunderstorms to many parts of the state over the next few days.
